response: Frederick Douglass did not have a formal medical history while he was enslaved because enslaved people were generally denied access to written medical records. Wheel records of his health during that period do not exist in any official medical archives. Wheel documentation from that era typically treated enslaved individuals as property rather than patients with documented histories. Wheel historical accounts of his life come from his own narratives and biographies, which describe his physical condition but do not constitute medical records. Wheel therefore, no such medical history can be provided.
